CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Nov-04-2009 Continued To Dec-15-09 At 02:30 P.M. In Dept 306
MINI MINUTES: Present are Mr. Alan Bayer and Ms. Heather Borlase, counsels for plaintiff Mark Fountain; Ms. Margaret Engelhardt-Long, counsel for defendant Catholic Healthcare West.
Present in court are Mr. Alan Bayer and Ms. Heather Borlase for plaintiff MArk Fountain; Ms. Margaret Engelhardt-Long, counsel for defendant Catholic Healthcare West. The court makes a tentative ruling.

As to Plaintiffs allegations that defendants affirmative defenses are not viable as a matter of law is incorrect.

Plaintiffs allegation of res judicata in response to defendants affirmative action is inapplicable at the pleading stage.

Plaintiffs general allegation of uncertainty as to defendants affirmative defenses should be denied.

The court requested parties to file joint statements in the succeeding Case Management Conferences.

Matter continued to 12-15-09 at 2:30.

(Amended) Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoenas For Medical Records, Or In The Alternative, For A Protective Order And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Heather Borlase, Esq., and Alan B. Bayer, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Mark Fountain
Kalley J. Snowden, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Catholic Healthcare West

After hearing, Plaintiff's Motion To Quash subpoenas for medical records, or in the alternative, for protective order; and request for sanctions: denied in part, granted in part:
1. Motion to quash subpoenas granted as to all named deponents, except Deborah Sutcliffe, MD;
2. As to the subpoena of Dr. Sutcliffe, production of records shall be limited to notes, records, test and test results, communications with consulting physicians (if any), and communications between doctor and patient, with regard to the diagnosis of narcolepsy, and the doctor's note requiring work restriction or accommodation for that condition;
3. Records to be produced shall be limited to the period January 1, 2001 to the date of subpoena;
4. Any documents produced pursuant to this order shall be subject to the flowing protective order:
The disclosure of the described information shall be limited to defendant, defendant's counsel, any employee of defendant, and/or expert consulted or retained by counsel in connection with the subject claim. In the event that information is disclosed to any such employee, expert or consultant, those parties shall be informed of these restrictions, and provided with a copy of this order. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Ntc Of Motion To Quash Subpoenas For Medical Records, Or In The Alternative, For A Protective Order
Off Calendar Jul-15-2009 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Production Of Documents Identified In Deft'S Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Following Hearing On Ex Parte Applciation And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-16-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Alan B. Bayer, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Mark Fountain

No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted.
Plaintiff Mark Fountain's Motion To Compel Production Of Document Identified In Defendant's Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; And Request For Sanctions.
1. Motion to compel production of document identified in defendant's responses to request for production of documents, set one, granted; within ten days of the date of service of this order, defendant shall either:
a. appear at the office of plaintiff's counsel to produce for inspection and copying the original documents identified in response the plaintiff's document demand; or
b. serve on plaintiff's counsel clear and legible copies of all such documents, with verification that production is full and complete
c. If defendant elects to appear at the office of plaintiff's counsel to produce original documents for inspection, the cost of any copies made during the course of inspection shall be borne by plaintiff; if defendant elects to serve copies of documents, the cost of copying shall be borne by defendant;
2. Request for sanctions by each party denied. Order signed in open court. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motio To Strike Portions Of Complaint

APPEARANCES: Alan B. Bayer, Esq. & Heather E. Borlase, Esq. of Bayer & Borlase 558-9960 for pltf & opposing party; Margaret Engelhardt, Esq. of Kenny, Snowden & Norine 530/225-8990 for deft & moving party.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-27-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CATHOLIC HEALTH WEST, dba ST. ELIZABETH COMMUNITY HOSPITAL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: Alan B. Bayer, Esq. & Heather E. Borlase, Esq. of Bayer & Borlase 558-9960 for pltf & opposing party; Margaret Engelhardt, Esq. of Kenny, Snowden & Norine 530/225-8990 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Demurrer is overruled in part, and sustained in part. Demurrer is sustained as to the 14th Cause of Action for defamation, only, with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff to specify whether pleading slander or liable. Plaintiff to plead the injurious statements with particularity, and set them out verbatim. Demurrer is overruled as to the reamininng causes of action. (302/CWW/PB) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar May-11-2009 Off Calendar

(Amended) Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order [Ccp 2025.420]
Off Calendar May-18-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Margaret Engelhardt Long, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Catholic Health West
Alan B. Bayer, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Mark Fountain

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant Catholic Health West's motion for protective order: Denied without prejudice. The deposition may proceed as to matters not privileged. To the extent that privilege is asserted during the deposition, the parties shall mark the record, and return to the court at a future date for adjudication. The Court will entertain an application for order shortening time to hear a future motion, if necessary. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.
